topic.what: Impact of layer-based security training on security quality of LLM-generated Java backend code
topic.how: Within-subject pre/post training comparison with 12 developers, counterbalanced task sets, manual security review
topic.why_matters: Addresses whether developer-side training can improve LLM output security without modifying the model itself

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total security findings across all severity levels
characteristics.evidence: Manual review by first and second authors across 24 submissions (12 pre, 12 post)
characteristics.context: 31.5% reduction; counterbalanced task sets to control for order effects

characteristics.evidence: Exact Wilcoxon signed-rank test p=0.0059 (Holm-adjusted p=0.0117)
characteristics.context: 38.2% reduction; primary endpoint; robust to exponential weighting scheme (p=0.0034)
Largest proportional improvement; critical for real-world impact

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rank-biserial correlation for paired severity-weighted change
characteristics.context: 9 of 12 participants improved; one worsened; two unchanged. Large uniform effect.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Median paired change in severity-weighted score (pre minus post)
characteristics.evidence: Exact Wilcoxon signed-rank test; median improvement across participants
characteristics.context: Per-participant improvement; within-subject design controls individual differences

characteristics.evidence: Largest reduction among weakness families; explicit prompting for boundary assumptions and ownership checks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Authentication, credential policy, and recovery findings
characteristics.evidence: Second largest family reduction; credential handling and recovery flows targeted in training
characteristics.context: 44.7% reduction; central failure mode in identity-centric systems

characteristics.what_it_measures: Session, request, and browser trust-boundary weakness count
characteristics.evidence: No change; operational controls and session handling not improved by prompting guidance
characteristics.context: 0% change; suggests reliance on framework defaults and deeper expertise

characteristics.what_it_measures: Weaknesses mapped to MA-CoT ecosystem library/framework layer
characteristics.evidence: Largest MA-CoT layer reduction; framework configuration and application-level decisions targeted
characteristics.context: 52.1% reduction; strongest effect at ecosystem layer where mitigations are expressible through prompting

good_for: Java Spring Boot identity-centric backend development
improving security quality of LLM-generated code through developer training
reducing authorization and authentication weaknesses via structured prompting
measuring security outcomes in controlled quasi-experimental settings
not_for: C/C++ low-level ecosystems where runtime misuse is prominent
Models requiring automated prompt optimization rather than manual training
Generalization to other languages/frameworks without replication
requires: Controlled environment with unified LLM interface
Pre/post paired design with counterbalanced tasks
Manual security review with dual independent assessors
Structured training package (instructional content + MA-CoT model + prompting scaffold)
